serif"},"children":"Single vs Dual vs Ultrawide: The Monitor Setup Guide"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/mia-collins","className":"flex items-center gap-2 object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","6 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"January 18, 2025"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"More screens doesn't always mean more productivity. Here's how to choose the monitor setup that actually matches your work."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-multi-monitor-myth","children":"The Multi-Monitor Myth"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"There's a widespread belief that more monitors automatically equals more productivity. Research suggests the reality is more complicated."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Adding a second monitor does measurably improve productivity for tasks involving frequent reference switching — comparing documents, coding with documentation open, monitoring dashboards. For tasks requiring sustained focus on a single thing, multiple monitors are often a distraction source."}}],["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The right setup depends on what you actually do."}}],["$","h2","4",{"id":"the-single-monitor-case","children":"The Single Monitor Case"}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single large, high-quality monitor in the 27–32\" range (ideally 4K) is underrated. It forces window discipline, reduces the temptation to multitask, and results in a cleaner, calmer desk."}}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a"]}],"$L1b","$L1c","$L1d"]}],"$L1e"]}]]}]
7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L1f",null,{"children":["$","$20",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@21"}]}]
9:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For writers, designers doing focused work, and anyone who finds dual monitors pulls their attention, this is often the best setup."}}]
a:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Best options</strong>: LG 27UK850-W (27\" 4K USB-C), Dell U2722D (27\" QHD), Samsung ViewFinity S8 (27\" 4K)."}}]
b:["$","h2","8",{"id":"the-dual-monitor-case","children":"The Dual Monitor Case"}]
c:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Two monitors work best when your work genuinely requires two parallel contexts — a primary working space and a reference/communication space."}}]
d:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The key is intentionality. Designate what lives on each monitor. Email and Slack live on the secondary monitor; work lives on the primary. Without that discipline, the second monitor becomes a distraction machine."}}]
e:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For the secondary monitor, you don't need top specs — a simple 24\" 1080p panel works fine."}}]
f:["$","h2","12",{"id":"the-ultrawide-case","children":"The Ultrawide Case"}]
10:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A 34\" or 38\" ultrawide (21:9 or 16:10 aspect ratio) gives you the screen real estate of dual monitors without the bezel in the middle of your view."}}]
11:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The lack of a seam is genuinely pleasant for creative work. The wide aspect ratio is excellent for video editing, photo editing, and coding."}}]
12:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Downsides: they're expensive, they require a GPU that can drive them, and some applications don't handle ultra-wide aspect ratios gracefully."}}]
13:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Best options</strong>: LG 34WN780 (34\" QHD), Dell U3423WE (34\" QHD with USB-C hub), Samsung Odyssey G9 (49\" if you want to go truly extreme)."}}]
14:["$","h2","17",{"id":"panel-technology","children":"Panel Technology"}]
15:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>IPS</strong>: Best colors and viewing angles. Slight glow in dark scenes. The right choice for most people."}}]
16:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>VA</strong>: Excellent contrast and deep blacks. Slightly slower response than IPS. Good for multimedia consumption and mixed use."}}]
17:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>OLED</strong>: Stunning picture quality, perfect blacks, expensive, possible burn-in with static content. Best for creative professionals and premium builds."}}]
18:["$","h2","21",{"id":"resolution-and-refresh-rate","children":"Resolution and Refresh Rate"}]
19:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For a desk monitor, 1440p (QHD) is the sweet spot — noticeably sharper than 1080p, less GPU-demanding than 4K. At 27\", 1440p looks excellent."}}]
1a:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For monitors used primarily for productivity (not gaming), a 60Hz refresh rate is perfectly adequate. The difference between 60Hz and 144Hz is much more noticeable in fast-paced games than in a text editor."}}]
1b:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Gear","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-orange-50 text-orange-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Gear"}],[["$","$L3","monitors",{"href":"/blog/tag/monitors","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","monitors"]}],["$","$L3","display",{"href":"/blog/tag/display","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","display"]}],["$","$L3","dual-monitor",{"href":"/blog/tag/dual-monitor","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","dual-monitor"]}],["$","$L3","peripherals",{"href":"/blog/tag/peripherals","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","peripherals"]}]]]}]
1c:["$","$L22",null,{"title":"Single vs Dual vs Ultrawide: The Monitor Setup Guide","slug":"monitor-setup-guide"}]
1d:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L23",null,{}]}]

25:["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M7 7h10v10","key":"1tivn9"}],["path",{"d":"M7 17 17 7","key":"1vkiza"}]],"className":"lucide-arrow-up-right","size":14}]
